---
title: "nitrix.linalg.spd"
generated: true
signature-hash: 4d33e318fb3a85e4
description: "Symmetric positive-definite manifold operations."
listing:
  id: nitrix-linalg-spd-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-linalg-spd/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.linalg.spd`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Symmetric positive-definite manifold operations.

Applying $\log$ / $\sqrt{\cdot}$ / a fractional power to the
eigenvalues of a symmetric positive-definite (SPD) matrix is numerically
unstable when *any* eigenvalue is at or below the machine-precision floor of
the input's dtype.

This module handles that instability through three design choices:

1. **Eigenvalue clipping is on by default.**  `eigvalue_clip='auto'`
   clips at $\max_i |\lambda_i| \cdot d \cdot \varepsilon$, the
   standard rank-truncation threshold (matching
   `numpy.linalg.matrix_rank`), where $d$ is the matrix
   dimension and $\varepsilon$ is the dtype's machine epsilon.
   Eigenvalues below the threshold are set to the threshold itself rather
   than to zero -- $\log(\mathrm{threshold})$ is finite, whereas
   $\log(0)$ is $-\infty$ and would contaminate everything
   downstream.
2. **No silent NaN filling.**  If the input is genuinely indefinite the
   operation is mathematically undefined, so the `NaN` is surfaced rather
   than silently replaced with `0`.
3. **The differentiability hook is explicit.**  Pass `psi > 0` to
   recondition the input before the eigendecomposition (perturbing
   degenerate eigenvalues so the eigendecomposition VJP does not blow up).
   This is a backward-only concern; for well-conditioned input the forward
   result is the same either way.

Public surface:

- [`symmap`](nitrix-linalg-spd/symmap.qmd) -- generic eigenvalue-function map.
- [`symlog`](nitrix-linalg-spd/symlog.qmd), [`symexp`](nitrix-linalg-spd/symexp.qmd), [`symsqrt`](nitrix-linalg-spd/symsqrt.qmd) -- the common cases.
- [`sympower`](nitrix-linalg-spd/sympower.qmd) -- arbitrary real power of an SPD matrix.
- [`tangent_project_spd`](nitrix-linalg-spd/tangent_project_spd.qmd) / [`cone_project_spd`](nitrix-linalg-spd/cone_project_spd.qmd) -- log-Euclidean
  tangent-space projection used in the Riemannian mean / connectivity
  embedding literature.
- [`mean_log_euclidean`](nitrix-linalg-spd/mean_log_euclidean.qmd) -- closed-form Fréchet mean under the
  log-Euclidean metric, $\exp(\operatorname{mean}_i \log X_i)$.
- [`mean_euclidean`](nitrix-linalg-spd/mean_euclidean.qmd) -- the trivial Euclidean mean, provided for
  symmetry with the SPD mean family.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
| `batch` | leading batch axes (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-linalg-spd-symbols}
:::
